PostToolUse.//: Frontend TypeScript edits → re-run tsc so type errors surface immediately instead of waiting for the next commit. Limited to 10 lines of output to keep the agent's context window clean; if a real error fires, we re-run the full check ourselves before /release.

hooks.command: if [ -n "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" ] && echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE 'frontend/.+\.(tsx?|ts)$'; then cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/frontend" && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -10 || true; fi

hooks.//: Backend Go edits → `go build ./...` from project root. Doesn't run tests (no test suite worth caching), just compile.
hooks.command: if [ -n "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" ] && echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && go build ./... 2>&1 | head -10 || true; fi

PostToolUse.//: Same checks fire after Write (new file) — covers the `New + immediate commit` path that Edit misses.

hooks.command: if [ -n "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" ] && echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE 'frontend/.+\.(tsx?|ts)$'; then cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/frontend" && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -10 || true; fi
if [ -n "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" ] && echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && go build ./... 2>&1 | head -10 || true; fi
